findings.message: The create_item route persists through db/item_service.add_item, a second, duplicate data-access layer for the Item entity. The repository module (db/repository.py) is already the established data layer and even exposes a create_item(session, name) helper. Routing the write through item_service while reads go through repository splits data-access for one entity across two parallel modules — inappropriate coupling and a duplicated data layer that will drift (e.g. clamping/ordering invariants enforced in repository are not shared by item_service).
findings.suggestion: Persist via the existing repository.create_item (or consolidate add_item into repository) so all Item data access flows through a single layer; delete db/item_service.py rather than maintaining a duplicate.

findings.message: Importing from ..db.item_service introduces a second data-access module alongside ..db.repository for the same Item entity. fetch_all_items is imported but never used, and unlike repository.list_items it performs an unbounded `session.query(Item).all()` with no limit — adopting it would reintroduce the unbounded-read risk repository.list_items was written to prevent.
findings.suggestion: Drop the item_service import; route all reads/writes through db/repository so the bounded-read invariant (MAX_PAGE_SIZE clamping) is preserved and there is one data layer.
